title: FLS Module 11: Suturing, Knot Tying, and Stapling

questions.text: What is the appropriate trocar size for introducing a standard curved SH needle through the cannula?
questions.explanation: Standard curved needles typically require a 10 to 12 mm trocar for direct introduction through the port without distortion or needle damage.

questions.text: What is the ideal suture length for intracorporeal knot tying?
questions.explanation: For intracorporeal suturing, about 15 cm (6 in) allows sufficient length for manipulation and tying while avoiding excess bulk in the operative field.

questions.text: What is the ideal suture length for extracorporeal knot tying techniques?
questions.explanation: A minimum of about 75 cm (30 in) is generally needed for extracorporeal knot tying to allow enough suture to reach and manipulate the knot outside the body.

questions.text: Which of the following is not recommended when introducing a suture needle into the abdomen?

answerOptions.text: A) Grasping the suture 5–10 mm from the needle
C) Grasping the needle directly for control
D) Backloading through the port when needed

questions.explanation: Grasping the needle directly when introducing it through a port increases the risk of bending the needle or injuring tissue. It is safer to grasp the suture close to the needle instead.

questions.text: Which type of suture is generally easier to handle and visualize for laparoscopic suturing?
questions.explanation: Braided sutures are more pliable and easier to tie due to reduced memory, and dyed versions improve visibility in a bloody field.

questions.text: What is a common advantage of using curved needle drivers facing opposite directions (e.g., left and right curves)?

answerOptions.text: A) They are easier to clean post-op
C) They improve ergonomics for knot tying

questions.explanation: Having concave curves that face each other allows smoother instrument handling and better ergonomics during knot tying, especially for intracorporeal techniques.

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding needle positioning for laparoscopic suturing?

answerOptions.text: A) The needle must always be positioned outside the field before pickup
B) The needle should be passed in a straight line across the field
C) A light glint indicates the needle is perpendicular to the light source
D) The assisting instrument should always release the needle before driving it

questions.explanation: A glint of light on the needle helps confirm that it is oriented correctly relative to the light source, which aids in accurate placement.

questions.text: Which maneuver best minimizes tissue trauma when placing a curved needle through tissue?

answerOptions.text: A) Linear thrust through the target
B) Gentle rotational motion that follows the needle curve
C) Twisting the wrist to drive the tip
D) Lifting the tissue upward before placing needle

questions.explanation: Rotating the needle in the direction of its curve allows it to pass smoothly and reduces tissue tearing and distortion.

questions.text: When passing a needle through tissue, the assisting instrument should:

answerOptions.text: A) Hold the needle driver firmly
C) Grasp the tissue first, then the needle tip
D) Hold the camera for stability

questions.explanation: The assistant should stabilize the tissue while the needle is placed, then help by grasping near the needle tip as it exits to guide it through.

questions.text: What is the main ergonomic guideline for ideal port placement during suturing?

answerOptions.text: A) Ports <5 cm apart for triangulation
C) Port distance ≥10 cm and elbows flexed 90°
D) Ports aligned midline for needle angles

questions.explanation: Ports spaced at least about 10 cm apart with the surgeon’s elbows flexed around 90° improves ergonomics, reduces fatigue, and enhances control during suturing.

questions.text: Which knot is most appropriate when precise tension and control is needed during tissue approximation?
questions.explanation: The sliding square knot allows gradual and precise tightening of the tissue, which is useful when delicate control of tension is required.

questions.text: Why is it important for surgeons to retain manual suturing skills despite availability of automated devices?

answerOptions.text: B) Instruments are rarely available in hospitals
C) Devices may fail or be unavailable in some situations
D) Most laparoscopic procedures are now done robotically

questions.explanation: Automated suturing or knot-tying devices can malfunction or may not be available, so surgeons must still be able to perform essential suturing steps manually.

questions.text: Which of the following is true regarding extracorporeal knot tying?

answerOptions.text: A) It places less tension on tissue than intracorporeal methods
B) It requires shorter suture than intracorporeal methods
C) It is more efficient for delicate or friable tissue
D) It generally applies more strain to tissues

questions.explanation: Extracorporeal knot tying is efficient but tends to place more tension on tissues as knots are pushed down from outside the abdomen.

questions.text: What distinguishes a Roeder’s knot from a standard square knot in laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: A) It is a single throw
C) It is entirely pre-tied and then pushed down
D) It uses barbed suture only

questions.explanation: Roeder’s knots are pre-tied extracorporeally and then slid down with a knot pusher, making them useful for certain ligating tasks.

questions.text: Which device is best suited for approximating the end of a transected fallopian tube?
questions.explanation: Pre-tied loops such as Roeder’s knot or an endoloop are efficient and effective for securing tubular structures like the appendix or fallopian tube.

questions.text: What is a potential drawback of using undyed suture material in laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: D) Poor visibility in a bloody field

questions.explanation: Undyed suture can blend into blood and surrounding tissue, making it harder to see and manipulate safely.

questions.text: Which cartridge color typically corresponds to 3–3.5 mm staple height for GI tissue (Ethicon Brand)?
questions.explanation: Blue cartridges (Ethicon Brand) contain staples in the 3–3.5 mm range and are commonly used for GI tract tissue, though color coding can vary by manufacturer.

questions.text: Staplers must be used with caution to avoid including extraneous tissue. What helps prevent this?

answerOptions.text: A) Placing a clip before stapling
B) Ensuring full articulation of stapler
C) Visualizing both tips of the staple cartridge
D) Using white cartridge for all applications

questions.explanation: Ensuring that both ends of the staple jaws are clearly seen helps confirm that only the intended tissue is between the jaws and prevents accidental inclusion of adjacent structures.

questions.text: Why should the needle be cut off after pulling suture back through the port in extracorporeal tying?

answerOptions.text: C) To allow Roeder's knot placement
D) To avoid tissue drag and facilitate knot formation

questions.explanation: Cutting the needle off allows the suture loop to be closed and prevents the needle from dragging on tissue, making extracorporeal knot formation easier and safer.

questions.text: What is the most versatile method of tissue approximation in laparoscopic surgery?
questions.explanation: Sutures can be used in the widest range of situations and allow very fine control of tissue approximation compared with clips, staples, or sealants alone.

questions.text: What is the primary advantage of using interrupted sutures over a continuous suture line in laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: C) Less suture material in the abdomen at one time

questions.explanation: Interrupted sutures are placed and tied one at a time, so there is less continuous suture length in the field, which reduces clutter and entanglement risk.

questions.text: Which technique allows curved needles to be introduced through 3–5 mm ports?

answerOptions.text: B) Pushing the needle through the valve while port remains in place
C) Backloading the port over the needle holder and introducing the needle through the port site
D) Twisting the needle to reduce diameter

questions.explanation: Backloading involves temporarily removing the port, sliding it over the instrument that holds the needle, and then reintroducing both through the same site to bring a curved needle in via a small port size.

questions.text: What is the role of an instrument placed between tissue and suture during extracorporeal withdrawal?

answerOptions.text: B) To act as a fulcrum and reduce tissue drag
D) To assist in extracorporeal knot tying

questions.explanation: Placing an instrument between the suture and tissue creates an angle that reduces friction and force on the tissue as the suture is pulled through, minimizing trauma.

questions.text: In forming a square knot with flat throws, which of the following is true?

answerOptions.text: A) A random sequence of wraps results in reliable knots
B) Only the dominant hand is used in wrapping
C) The C-loop should originate from the left, and the wrap should be clockwise
D) Flat throws are only needed in continuous sutures

questions.explanation: A consistent technique—such as forming a C-loop from the left and wrapping clockwise—helps produce true square knots with flat, secure throws.
